acceptance_rules: Existing directory selectors produce source refs with path, repo, sha256, and expansion handle.
Missing selectors produce explicit REJECT entries, not silent omissions.
Receipts include graph and freshness hashes for the selected path manifest.
Raw private payload is not included; downstream tools expand refs locally.
The receipt can replace or refine a Telos domain-envelope workspace_context layer.
negative_rules: Do not treat repo-root context as path-scoped context.
Do not promote missing paths such as build-universe as covered.
Do not call the bridge implemented until Index emits the receipt natively or an adapter emits equivalent receipts.
contract.purpose: Produce source-ref-only context receipts for explicit path selectors under a workspace root.

evidence.description: BuildLang source refs and live buildc corpus receipt.
BuildLang source receipt joined into domain envelope.
Index focus bridge requirement from live path probes.
Build Color software proof kit with bounded metrics.
Color calibration and rendering market map.
Frontier problem and megatool opportunity map.

growth_vectors.next_experiment: Emit an IndexPathSelectorReceipt fixture for buildlang and compiler, while rejecting build-universe.
growth_vectors.why: Large monorepos and scientific codebases need proof packets around selected subsystems, not only repo roots.

growth_vectors.next_experiment: Define a telos proof build command over one existing packet and one BuildLang receipt.
growth_vectors.why: The research surface needs one command that joins Gather source, Index context, Forum route, action receipts, and Crucible verdicts.

growth_vectors.next_experiment: Expose rejected claims alongside MATCH claims in a public-safe proof packet viewer.
growth_vectors.why: Buyers trust systems that refuse overclaims; repeated REJECT fixtures are a product asset, not internal ceremony.

growth_vectors.next_experiment: Recheck the pass 0011 Build Color proof kit through the same path-selector contract shape.
growth_vectors.why: Color, rendering, display, and perception evidence is under-integrated with AI and research proof systems.

growth_vectors.next_experiment: Bind one AI4Science source map row to a claim packet with explicit unverified boundaries.
growth_vectors.why: AI4Science tools generate claims across literature, model runs, proof attempts, and experiments; the market gap is claim-level verification continuity.

non_promotion_statement: Pass 0077 defines a contract and ranked hypotheses. It does not implement Index path selection, prove market uniqueness, validate AI4Science discoveries, calibrate hardware, prove Julia replacement, or promote a natural law.

primary_30_day_push.hypothesis: Ship the Index path-selector receipt and wrap the live buildc corpus receipt into a public BuildLang proof-packet demo first.
primary_30_day_push.reason: It has the strongest current proof advantage and demo readiness while directly improving the shared substrate needed by color/rendering and AI4Science packets.

product_motions.label: BuildLang/buildc proof packets
product_motions.primary_risk: Compiler ecosystem adoption is hard; the first wedge must sell accountability, not broad Julia replacement.
product_motions.rationale: Best near-term proof surface: live buildc corpus receipt, domain-envelope join, and a crisp path-selector gap.

product_motions.label: AI4Science research proof packets
product_motions.primary_risk: A research packet that cannot satisfy domain reviewers becomes ceremony; start with formal/math sources before wet-lab claims.
product_motions.rationale: Highest mission upside and strong market need, but independent domain validation remains harder than compiler/color demos.

product_motions.label: Color/rendering measurement packets
product_motions.primary_risk: Hardware calibration claims need sensor-backed receipts before promotion.
product_motions.rationale: Distinctive trust layer with bounded software metrics and clear non-hardware-calibration boundaries.
